skill.name: ux-prototyping
skill.description: Create interactive single-file HTML prototypes from UX specifications to validate user flows, screen states, and interaction patterns.
skill.summary: Build single-file HTML prototypes for UX flow validation.
skill.author: Emz1998

security_audit.summary: AI review confirmed one real browser-side XSS risk in the prototype template where user-entered item data is rendered through innerHTML. The external command, hardcoded URL, weak cryptography, obfuscation, and reconnaissance alerts are false positives caused by markdown examples, JavaScript template literals, SVG namespace URLs, CSS, and local demo identifiers. No prompt injection, malicious intent, credential access, or data exfiltration was found.

medium_findings.title: DOM XSS Risk in Prototype Template Rendering
medium_findings.description: The template stores user-entered item descriptions and renders the item list with innerHTML. Because item fields are interpolated into markup, prototype users can inject HTML or script into the generated page.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The affected values come from form input and are inserted through innerHTML without escaping. This is a direct client-side XSS pattern in a reusable prototype template.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Shell Execution Indicators
low_findings.description: The flagged backticks are markdown code fences in the skill instructions or JavaScript template literals and CSS selectors in the browser template. I found no shell command execution or command injection path.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The contexts are HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and markdown, not Ruby or a shell. There is no API or runtime mechanism that executes operating system commands.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Hardcoded URL Indicators
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL findings are SVG xmlns attributes inside inline icons. They identify the SVG namespace and do not perform network requests.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Each URL appears as an SVG namespace in static markup. There are no fetch, XMLHttpRequest, beacon, image tracking, or remote script loads.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Cryptography, Reconnaissance, and Obfuscation Alerts
low_findings.description: The flagged lines are ordinary prototype labels, CSS, placeholder copy, local DOM IDs, Date.now demo IDs, textContent assignments, and array join rendering. I found no cryptographic implementation, host reconnaissance, or concealed payload.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Review of the surrounding HTML and JavaScript shows local UI prototype behavior only. The suspicious tokens are lexical matches, not security-sensitive operations.

dangerous_patterns.title: DOM XSS Risk in Prototype Template Rendering
dangerous_patterns.description: The template stores user-entered item descriptions and renders the item list with innerHTML. Because item fields are interpolated into markup, prototype users can inject HTML or script into the generated page.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The affected values come from form input and are inserted through innerHTML without escaping. This is a direct client-side XSS pattern in a reusable prototype template.

content.value_statement: Teams often need to test a user flow before investing in production code. This skill turns UX specifications into interactive single-file HTML prototypes for fast validation.
actual_capabilities: Reads a UX specification from specs/architecture/ux.md or a user-provided path.
Creates a single HTML file with inline CSS and JavaScript.
Models screens, navigation, modals, forms, and simple local state.
Includes empty, loading, error, success, and partial states when relevant.
Uses responsive defaults for mobile-focused UX validation.
Saves the prototype to a named HTML output file.
limitations: It prioritizes UX flow fidelity over pixel-perfect visual design.
It does not create a backend, database, authentication service, or API integration.
Generated templates should escape untrusted content before public sharing.
It does not replace live usability testing with real participants.

use_cases.title: Validate a Product Flow
use_cases.description: Turn a written UX flow into a clickable prototype for stakeholder review before engineering starts.

use_cases.title: Explore Interaction States
use_cases.description: Create a prototype that shows empty, loading, error, and success states for a planned experience.

use_cases.title: Review Scope Before Build
use_cases.description: Use a single-file prototype to check navigation, screen coverage, and edge cases before implementation.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Prototype
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request with an existing UX document.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an interactive UX prototype from specs/architecture/ux.md. Include the main screens, primary navigation, and core task flow.

prompt_templates.title: Prototype a Specific Flow
prompt_templates.scenario: Focused request for one user journey.
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a single-file HTML prototype for the onboarding flow in my UX spec. Include validation, empty states, and success confirmation.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Alternate Flows
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for UX decision support.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a prototype that shows two alternate checkout flows from the UX spec. Make the differences easy to test and review.

prompt_templates.title: Stress Test Edge Cases
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for deeper UX validation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Build an interactive prototype for the dashboard flow. Include loading, error, partial data, permissions, and recovery paths for each screen.

output_examples.input: A UX spec describes a mobile onboarding flow with sign up, profile setup, and a completion screen.
output_examples.output: A clickable HTML prototype with welcome, account creation, validation errors, profile setup, and success screens.

output_examples.input: A team wants to review dashboard empty states and item creation before implementation.
output_examples.output: A single-page prototype with an empty dashboard, create-item modal, populated list, detail view, and settings screen.

output_examples.input: A designer needs to evaluate error recovery in a search flow.
output_examples.output: An interactive prototype showing search input, loading feedback, no-results state, network error state, and retry behavior.

best_practices: Provide a clear UX specification with screens, states, and primary tasks.
Ask for realistic sample content so stakeholders can judge hierarchy and labels.
Review generated prototypes for safe text escaping before sharing with untrusted users.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill to build production application code.
Do not skip edge states when the goal is workflow validation.
Do not treat visual polish as more important than task completion paths.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates interactive single-file HTML prototypes for validating user flows and screen behavior.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it require a UX spec?
faq.answer: A UX spec is recommended, but a detailed prompt with screens and flows can also work.

faq.question: Can the prototype connect to real services?
faq.answer: No. The intended output uses local browser interactions and simulated state.

faq.question: Is the generated HTML production ready?
faq.answer: No. It is for UX validation and should be reviewed before any production use.

faq.question: What security caution applies?
faq.answer: Prototype code should escape user-entered text before sharing it with untrusted users.
